description: Set inside the palatial Palazzo Venart on the Grand Canal, Glam is Venice's most ambitious dining room and the city's only two-Michelin-star address. The experience combines high-concept contemporary Italian cooking with polished luxury-hotel service and a serene garden-and-canal setting that feels unusually secluded for central Venice.
highlights: Venice's leading fine-dining table in a Grand Canal palace with two Michelin stars and a highly refined tasting-menu format.
sources: Michelin Guide; Enrico Bartolini official website; Palazzo Venart MICHELIN hotel page

description: Overlooking Piazza San Marco from one of Venice's most storied dining addresses, Quadri pairs historic grandeur with a sharply updated culinary identity under the Alajmo group. Philippe Starck's redesign, the room's theatrical sense of place, and a refined lagoon-focused menu make it one of the city's most complete luxury dining experiences.
highlights: A grand Michelin-starred restaurant above St Mark's Square with design pedigree, Venetian glamour, and serious culinary depth.
sources: Michelin Guide; 50 Best Discovery; Condé Nast Traveler; Time Out; Quadri official website

description: On the lagoon island of Mazzorbo, Venissa delivers one of Venice's most distinctive destination dinners inside a walled vineyard with a medieval bell tower. The cuisine is deeply tied to the lagoon, sustainability, and local terroir, yet the overall experience feels rarefied enough for international luxury travelers seeking something beyond the main historic center.
highlights: Michelin-starred island destination set in a vineyard, combining lagoon-driven cooking, romance, and a uniquely Venetian sense of place.
sources: Michelin Guide; 50 Best Discovery; Condé Nast Traveler; Venissa official website

description: Oro sits within the legendary Hotel Cipriani on Giudecca, where a circular dining room and lagoon-facing outlook frame one of Venice's classic fine-dining nights out. The food is contemporary and elegant, but the larger draw for luxury travelers is the combination of Belmond glamour, calm, and a sense of old-world occasion.
highlights: Michelin-starred dining at Hotel Cipriani with lagoon views, classic luxury service, and a polished contemporary Italian menu.
sources: Michelin Guide; Belmond official website; Condé Nast Traveler
hotel_name_hint: Hotel Cipriani, A Belmond Hotel, Venice

description: Wisteria brings a more intimate, contemporary form of fine dining to central Venice, with tasting menus that translate regional seasonality into a modern urban format. It is a strong choice for affluent diners who want Michelin-level precision without the ceremony of a grand hotel or historic institution.
highlights: A compact Michelin-starred dining room with modern tasting menus and a more understated, contemporary luxury feel.
sources: Michelin Guide; TheFork Michelin listings; Wisteria official website

description: Local is one of the most compelling modern restaurants in Venice, with a minimalist room, a highly regarded cellar, and a serious commitment to seasonal lagoon ingredients. It appeals to diners who want Michelin-level cooking in a more design-conscious, less formal setting than the city's grande dame addresses.
highlights: Michelin-starred contemporary Venetian restaurant with minimalist design, deep wine program, and strong local sourcing.
sources: Michelin Guide; Apple Maps; Local official website

description: Reached by boat on Isola delle Rose, Agli Amici Dopolavoro offers one of the most escapist luxury dining experiences in the Venetian lagoon. The setting inside JW Marriott Venice Resort & Spa gives it resort glamour, while the cooking supplies enough precision and ambition to justify the journey for destination diners.
highlights: Boat-accessed Michelin-starred dining on a private island resort, ideal for a special-occasion lagoon escape.
sources: Michelin Guide; JW Marriott Venice official website; TheFork Michelin listings
